name: network-trust-registry
description: Cross-channel behavioral fingerprinting, on-chain trust registry, graduated ban-evasion decisions, admin ops workflow, and per-channel security Q&A wizard
goals: Prevent ban evasion via device and behavioral fingerprint matching
Anchor verified fingerprints on a shared on-chain registry with category-weighted reputation
Auto-deny high-confidence evasion; pending admin review for uncertain risk only
Let channel admins configure security via private bot Q&A (/security)
Support hybrid reinstatement (local unban, decay, appeals)
nonGoals: End-user login, passwords, or email accounts
technicalNotes: Extend matching.py, models.py, handlers.py, blockchain.py — do not replace verify flow. Profile.status already exists. contracts/ is empty; deploy.js expects BanRegistry.sol. Phase order: local policy + ban evasion → ops + wizard → chain → network score → reinstatement.
_priority_order: 2. Dual-threshold matching + admin ops Telegram flow
_step_size: One logical change per commit. Quality over speed.

requirements.description: Add standardized ban category and severity enums; extend Ban model and record_ban() to persist them
acceptance: BanCategory enum includes spam, solicitation, scam_fraud, harassment, bot_abuse, impersonation, nsfw, raid_coordination, other
BanSeverity enum includes low, medium, high, permanent
Ban ORM has category and severity columns with sensible defaults for legacy rows
record_ban() accepts category and severity and persists them
pytest tests/test_ban_taxonomy.py passes

test.description: Add tests/test_ban_taxonomy.py: enum values, record_ban persists category/severity, migration/default for existing bans

implement.description: Create singulr/domain/ban_taxonomy.py; add Ban.category and Ban.severity to models.py; extend record_ban() signature

refactor.description: Keep reason text as human note; ensure admin API list_bans returns category/severity

verification: New ban row has non-null category and severity
test_bans.py still passes after updates

requirements.description: Add ChannelSecuritySettings model and get_effective_channel_policy() loader with env fallbacks
acceptance: ChannelSecuritySettings table keyed by channel_id with preset, thresholds, network_registry_mode, admin_ops_chat_id
get_effective_channel_policy(session, channel_id) returns defaults from config when no row exists
Policy dataclass includes ban_evasion_auto_deny_threshold and local_similarity_flag_threshold
pytest tests/test_channel_policy.py passes

test.description: Add tests/test_channel_policy.py: defaults, persisted overrides, field types

verification: DB row overrides env for same channel_id
Missing row uses balanced preset defaults

requirements.description: Implement dual-threshold ban evasion: high similarity to banned profile on new telegram_user_id → BLOCK; medium → PENDING

patterns: check_known_bad keystroke loop returns FLAG at >= keystroke_similarity_threshold

acceptance: Decision.PENDING exists (or FLAG documented as pending-only) for medium-confidence ban-evasion
New user_id with keystroke similarity >= ban_evasion_auto_deny_threshold against banned profile returns BLOCK
Similarity between flag and auto-deny thresholds against banned profile returns PENDING not BLOCK
check_known_bad accepts optional EffectivePolicy from channel_policy
pytest tests/test_matching.py includes high-confidence auto-deny and medium pending cases

test.description: Extend test_matching.py: seed banned profile on user A, verify user B with high vs medium keystroke similarity

implement.description: Add Decision.PENDING; split similarity branches using policy thresholds; only treat as ban-evasion when matched_ban links to different telegram_user_id

refactor.description: Fix stylometry loop wrong matched_ban attribution noted in overnight-improve audit

verification: High match → BLOCK with ban_evasion factor in risk_factors
Medium match → PENDING/FLAG without auto-ban path

requirements.description: Admin ops Telegram workflow: separate ops chat, Permit/Deny for pending, audit-only message for auto-deny
acceptance: log_to_ops_channel() posts to admin_ops_chat_id from channel policy or ADMIN_OPS_CHAT_ID env
Pending verification posts Permit and Deny callbacks (permit_/deny_ prefix)
Auto-deny BLOCK posts audit message without Permit button
Permit grants access and DMs user; Deny DMs denial reason

test.description: Add tests/test_bot_ops_workflow.py mocking telegram send_message and grant_access for pending and auto-deny paths

implement.description: Extend apply_verification_decision for pending vs block; add permit_/deny_ handlers; audit formatter for BAN_EVASION auto-deny

refactor.description: Reuse format helpers from test_log_format patterns

verification: Pending path does not call ban_member until Deny
Auto-deny path calls ban_member and notify_user_result denied

acceptance: submit calls check_known_bad with channel policy from token channel_id
PENDING/FLAG result returns decision pending to bot payload
BLOCK ban-evasion triggers auto-deny bot path not pending

test.description: Extend test_api_verify.py with mocked matching results for PENDING and BLOCK ban evasion

requirements.description: Network reputation scoring service integrated into check_known_bad with channel policy and category auto-reject
acceptance: compute_network_score(active_bans, policy) weights by category severity
scam_fraud permanent active ban causes BLOCK when network_registry_mode is read
Score between review_min and reject_min yields PENDING
Score above reject_min yields BLOCK
network_registry_mode off skips chain reputation fetch

requirements.description: Hybrid reinstatement: local unban with overturn event, time decay for low/medium categories, appeal record stub

apply_decay() reduces score contribution for low/medium bans older than configured months
permanent categories skip automatic decay
AppealRecord model stores pending appeals
POST /api/admin/appeals or documented bot command creates appeal
